source.definition: describing a person who lacks courage or is easily frightened

explanation.sourceText: The English adjective "cowardly" derives from the noun "coward," which comes from Old French "coart," meaning a person who is timid or fearful. The root is related to the idea of turning away or retreating in fear.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „cowardly” derivă de la substantivul „coward”, care provine din franceza veche „coart”, însemnând o persoană timidă sau fricoasă. Rădăcina este legată de ideea de a se întoarce sau retrage din frică.

source.additional: Cowardice is often viewed negatively and can lead to social exclusion in challenging situations.

target.definition: eine Person, die mutlos oder ängstlich ist

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word „laș“ probably comes from the Slavic „laz“ (to hide) and means lacking courage or cowardly.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „laș“ provine probabil din slavă „laz“ (a se ascunde) și înseamnă lipsit de curaj sau fricos.

target.additional: Laș wird oft verwendet, um jemanden zu beschreiben, der in riskanten Situationen ausweicht oder nicht den Mut hat, sich zu behaupten.

examples.sourceText: He is too cowardly to express his opinion.
examples.targetText: Este prea laș ca să își exprime opinia.

examples.sourceText: Cowardly people often do not dare to take risks.
examples.targetText: Oamenii lași nu îndrăznesc de obicei să își asume riscuri.

examples.sourceText: It is cowardly to run away when things get difficult.
examples.targetText: Este laș să fugi când devine greu.

examples.sourceText: Sometimes we have to overcome our cowardly fears.
examples.targetText: Uneori trebuie să trecem peste fricile noastre lașe.

examples.sourceText: A cowardly decision is never the right one.
examples.targetText: O decizie lașă nu este niciodată corectă.

learningTips.text: Remember 'cowardly' sounds like 'cow' + 'ardly'—imagine a scared cow running away to recall the meaning of 'laș'.
Think of the phrase 'Don't be a coward' to connect 'cowardly' with 'laș' as someone lacking courage.
The Romanian word 'laș' sounds like 'lash'—imagine someone too scared to 'lash out' to remember it means cowardly.
Associate 'laș' with 'lack' of courage to help recall its meaning as cowardly or timid.
